name: La Braguía and Pas Greenway Loop
name_original: Renedo de Piélagos, Río Pas, Selaya, Vega de Pas, Puente Viesgo – ruta circular
description: This difficult 58.8-mile road cycling route takes you through the scenic Valles Pasiegos, featuring significant climbs and cultural landmark
region_intro: Road cycling around Piélagos offers diverse landscapes, from coastal paths along the "Costa Quebrada" to routes traversing river valleys and varied inland topography. The region features mostly paved surfaces, making it suitable for road cycling. Routes often include significant elevation gains, providing challenging climbs and descents. The area's commitment to enhancing cycling infrastructure, such as the upcoming Pas river cycle path, further supports its appeal for outdoor activities. Best road cycling routes around Piélagos The most popular road cycling route is La Braguía and Pas Greenway Loop, a 58.8 miles (94.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 31 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 1,100 meters of elevation gain, offering a demanding ride through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto del Caracol – Pas Greenway loop from Renedo de Piélagos, a difficult 58.2 miles (93.7 km) path. This route includes over 1,300 meters of climbing, providing extensive views and a significant physical challenge. Local road cyclists also love the Covalruyo Viewpoint – Portillo Lunada loop from Parbayón, a 77.2 miles (124.3 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes with over 2,500 meters of elevation gain, often completed in about 6 hours 50 minutes. Road cycling around Piélagos is defined by coastal paths, river valleys, and significant elevation gains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Piélagos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Piélagos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Piélagos?
A: Piélagos offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 25 options available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult, ensuring there's a suitable challenge for most cyclists.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Piélagos?
A: While many routes in Piélagos feature significant elevation gains and are rated moderate to difficult, there are still options for less experienced riders. The region is developing new infrastructure, such as the upcoming cycle and pedestrian path along the Pas river, which will offer more accessible and scenic options ideal for families or beginners once completed.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on road cycling routes in Piélagos?
A: Road cycling in Piélagos offers a diverse range of landscapes, from the dramatic coastal views of the 'Costa Quebrada' to tranquil river valleys along the Pas river. You'll also encounter varied inland topography, including challenging climbs and descents through mountainous terrain, providing a rich visual experience.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Piélagos?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Piélagos are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004556769/la-braguia-and-pas-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Braguía and Pas Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20181685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Caracol – Pas Greenway loop from Renedo de Piélagos&lt;/a&gt; are excellent circular options for experienced riders.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Piélagos offers several scenic viewpoints and natural attractions. You can enjoy stunning vistas from places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1511383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Ballota&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3397254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Llen and Peña Cabarga Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20178867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covalruyo Viewpoint – Portillo Lunada loop from Parbayón&lt;/a&gt; also promises diverse landscapes and memorable sights.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about their experience in Piélagos?
A: The road cycling routes in Piélagos are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 5.0 stars from over 15 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the varied terrain, challenging climbs, and the beautiful coastal and riverine scenery that defines the region.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Piélagos?
A: Piélagos offers good road cycling conditions for much of the year. The region's diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to inland routes, can be enjoyed across seasons. However, for the most comfortable experience, spring and autumn generally provide milder temperatures and pleasant weather for tackling the significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging road cycling routes for advanced riders?
A: Absolutely. Piélagos is known for its demanding routes with substantial elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40354162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Alisas – Collado de Asón loop from Parbayón&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, covers over 142 km with more than 2,400 meters of climbing, making it a significant challenge for very good fitness levels.

Q: What is the typical duration for road cycling routes in Piélagos?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and elevation. Shorter, moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33963613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Corrales de Buelna loop from Zurita&lt;/a&gt; can take around 2-3 hours. More challenging and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20178867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covalruyo Viewpoint – Portillo Lunada loop from Parbayón&lt;/a&gt;, can require 6-7 hours or more to complete.

Q: Is there good parking available near the road cycling trails in Piélagos?
A: While specific parking areas for each trail are not detailed, Piélagos is committed to sustainable tourism and outdoor activities. As a general rule, you can often find parking in the towns and villages that serve as starting points for many routes, such as Boo de Piélagos, Renedo de Piélagos, or Parbayón.

Q: Does Piélagos have a focus on sustainable tourism for cyclists?
A: Yes, Piélagos emphasizes sustainable tourism and the preservation of its natural environment. The municipality aims to balance visitor enjoyment with the quality of life for its residents, ensuring that the natural beauty and cycling infrastructure are well-maintained and respected for future generations.
tips: A road between Saro and Bárcena de Carriedo. It is in good condition and with beautiful green fields and nice views.

Also known as El Puente Rual, the aqueduct is next to the Vía Verde del Pas. It was built in 1885 in order to supply drinking water to the city of Santander. It is an ideal place to take a short break – there is shade, benches and tables. In front there is a bar and a fountain.

German-made locomotive that entered service in 1913 to transport iron ores and limestone from the Camargo mines to its factory. It is located on the greenway, near the train station, converted into an exhibition hall and tourist information office.
A bridge that passes over the Pas River located between the towns of Oruña and Arce. It was built at the end of the 16th century and is declared an Asset of Cultural Interest.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging circular route is designed for race bikes and typically takes around 4 hours and 31 minutes to complete. This duration is for continuous cycling and doesn't include stops for rest, food, or sightseeing.

faqs.question: Is this circular route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and requires a very good fitness level. With nearly 95 kilometers and over 1,140 meters of elevation gain, it's best suited for experienced road cyclists looking for a substantial challenge.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The majority of the route is on paved roads, including national roads and streets, totaling about 93 kilometers. However, be prepared for some segments that might be unpaved and more challenging to ride. The Valles Pasiegos region is known for significant climbs and descents.

faqs.question: What are some notable sights or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: As you cycle through the Valles Pasiegos, you'll encounter beautiful natural scenery and traditional architecture. Specific highlights on the route include the Secondary Road Between Bárcena de Carriedo and Saro, the Santiurde de Toranzo Aqueduct, the Reyerma Steam Locomotive, and the Puente de Arce.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Valles Pasiegos region is known for its verdant landscapes, which are particularly beautiful in spring and early summer. These seasons often offer pleasant temperatures for cycling, though it's always wise to check the local weather forecast before you go, especially for mountain passes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this circular route?
faqs.answer: Since this is a circular route passing through several towns like Renedo de Piélagos, Selaya, Vega de Pas, and Puente Viesgo, you have multiple options for starting points. Look for public parking areas within these towns, especially near the beginning or end of the route in Puente Viesgo or Renedo de Piélagos.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily on public roads and is designed for road cycling. While there are no specific restrictions against dogs, it's generally not recommended to bring dogs on a challenging, long-distance road cycling route due to traffic and the physical demands on the animal.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, this road cycling route traverses public roads and does not require any special permits or entrance fees. You are free to enjoy the ride through the Cantabrian landscape.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other paths. Notably, about 13% of the route overlaps with the Vía Verde del Pas, a converted railway line that offers flat, traffic-free sections along the river. You'll also briefly cross parts of the Camino Natural del Valle de Toranzo. Ruta de Ontaneda.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities to experience local culture or gastronomy along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The route passes through several towns in the Valles Pasiegos, a region renowned for its gastronomy. You'll find opportunities to sample traditional dishes and local products like sobaos and quesadas in villages like Selaya and Vega de Pas. Keep an eye out for traditional 'cabañas pasiegas' in the countryside.

faqs.question: What should I consider bringing for this challenging ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, it's essential to be well-prepared. Bring plenty of water and nutrition, as well as repair kits for your race bike. Appropriate cycling attire, including layers for changing weather, and sun protection are also recommended. A GPS device with the route loaded is highly advisable.

waypoints.name: Secondary Road Between Bárcena de Carriedo and Saro
